patterns.robustness_guard: The weak model emits semantic slots only; deterministic compiler maps slots to known tables and columns.
patterns.runtime_effect: Shadow-only weak-model evaluation; packaged SQL_FIRST_API_VERIFY unchanged.
patterns.weak_model_benefit: Reduces burden on a weak model to memorize physical table and column names.

patterns.adopted_pattern: Schema-aware structured SQL intent instead of raw SQL
patterns.robustness_guard: Raw LLM SQL is not executed; generated SQL is deterministic and validated.
patterns.runtime_effect: Shadow-only weak-model evaluation; packaged SQL_FIRST_API_VERIFY unchanged.
patterns.weak_model_benefit: Converts weak-model semantic interpretation into safer executable actions.

patterns.robustness_guard: Invalid API-only choices for local snapshot questions are rejected and corrected to SQL-first slots.
patterns.runtime_effect: Shadow-only weak-model evaluation; packaged SQL_FIRST_API_VERIFY unchanged.
patterns.weak_model_benefit: Catches common weak-model tool-choice errors before execution.

patterns.robustness_guard: Only SELECT/read-only, known-table, known-column SQL can execute through the scaffold.
patterns.weak_model_benefit: Preserves safety even when the weak model misreads the prompt.

patterns.robustness_guard: Each stage records semantic slots, compiler output, tool calls, and evidence-grounded answer state.
patterns.runtime_effect: Diagnostic-only reports; final submission format unchanged.
patterns.weak_model_benefit: Makes scaffold lift measurable rather than treating the weak model as an opaque answer generator.

summary: The implementation borrows lightweight design patterns conceptually, without importing large frameworks or changing packaged runtime behavior.
